name: Wallonia (Belgium)

colors.symbolism: the heraldic gold of the field

description: A gold field with the Coq hardi (Bold Rooster), a stylised red rooster with one foot raised, at the centre, facing the hoist.
history: Designed by painter Pierre Paulus in 1913 for the Walloon Movement; adopted as the regional flag of the French Community of Belgium on 3 July 1991 and (with the same design) of the Walloon Region on 23 July 1998. The colours derive from the historic arms of the Principality of Liege.

features.role: Coq hardi (Bold Rooster), red rooster facing the hoist with one foot raised
features.symbolism: the rooster of the Walloon Movement; designed by Pierre Paulus in 1913, representing Wallonia's bold, watchful character
